hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3 and TM5/TM6, while avoiding off-target (SSTR3/SSTR5) binding through steric differentiation. This should increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 complementarity without compromising affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating high-affinity binders but insufficient structural quality and docking scores are limiting success. A stricter ddG threshold and targeted mutations at non-pharmacophore positions are needed to improve selectivity and structural quality.

proposed_changes.rationale: To filter out candidates with weaker binding affinities and retain only the most stable binders.
To guide mutations at non-pharmacophore positions that disrupt off-target interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting the conserved pharmacophore positions.
